categories.title: For the person who just moved to Burlington
categories.intro: A few ways to make a new address feel like home.

gifts.name: Common Deer
gifts.why: A giclée print of the actual topography around the lake and the Intervale — the kind of thing that goes up in a first apartment before the furniture arrives.

gifts.name: Homeport
gifts.item: Pour-over coffee maker with permanent filter
gifts.why: Homeport has stocked Burlington kitchens for 40 years across four floors on Church Street, and a pour-over rig is the first thing most new arrivals realize they forgot to pack.

gifts.name: Danforth Pewter
gifts.why: Cast, spun, and polished at Danforth's workshop in Middlebury — a small, permanent object that says the move is official.

gifts.name: Crow Bookshop
gifts.item: Gift card and a Vermont field guide
gifts.price: $25 gift card, plus $18–$24 for most Vermont guidebooks
gifts.why: Since 1995, Crow has stocked a full shelf of Vermont-specific nonfiction, from hiking guides to local history, next to its used and new fiction stacks.

gifts.name: Thirty-odd
gifts.price: $11 for a sticker four-pack; $15–$40 for maker goods
gifts.why: About 30 resident makers stock this Pine Street co-op themselves, so what's on the shelf changes with the season — good for someone who wants their new kitchen to look like it belongs here.

categories.title: Food that ships and edible Vermont
categories.intro: Burlington-area flavors that travel well — or disappear quickly at home.

gifts.name: Lake Champlain Chocolates
gifts.item: Five Star Bar Collection, six-piece box
gifts.where: 750 Pine St or 65 Church St; ships nationwide
gifts.why: The dark-chocolate-and-caramel Five Star bar has been the chocolatier's signature item since the 1980s, and the flagship Pine Street store still runs factory tours behind the retail counter.

gifts.name: Runamok
gifts.item: Best Sellers Maple Syrup Gift Box, three 250ml bottles
gifts.where: Online and at Burlington specialty grocers
gifts.why: Runamok barrel-ages and infuses its syrup with things like cinnamon and elderberry — a step up from the jug of syrup most people already have.

gifts.name: Shelburne Farms Store
gifts.why: The cheddar is made from raw milk from the farm's own Brown Swiss herd on the shore of Lake Champlain, aged in multiple stages so the gift set doubles as a tasting flight.

gifts.name: Ben & Jerry's
gifts.item: Original scoop shop pint gift card or merch
gifts.why: This is the gas-station-turned-scoop-shop where Ben and Jerry opened their first store in 1978 — more of a Burlington landmark than the Waterbury factory most tourists default to.

gifts.name: Onyx Tonics
gifts.why: Onyx runs its menu like a tasting room, rotating six-plus single-origin coffees from small U.S. roasters each week — Food & Wine named it one of the country's top 100 coffee shops.

gifts.name: Speeder & Earl's
gifts.why: Speeder's has roasted its own beans on Pine Street since 1993, well before the current wave of South End coffee shops opened around it.

categories.title: For kids
categories.intro: Things to keep, wear, and do around the lake.

gifts.name: Vermont Teddy Bear
gifts.price: $70; up to $120 with outfits and accessories
gifts.why: Made at the Shelburne factory since 1981, with a bear hospital on-site for repairs, so this isn't a bear that gets replaced when an ear comes loose — it gets fixed.

gifts.name: ECHO, Leahy Center for Lake Champlain
gifts.why: The touch tanks and Lake Champlain exhibits sit right on the waterfront, so a membership works as a standing invitation rather than a one-time outing.

gifts.name: Skida
gifts.where: Online and at Skirack or Common Deer
gifts.why: Skida's headwear is still sewn in Burlington, the same city where 16-year-old Corinne Prevot started the company in 2008 — a kid-sized hat literally made a few blocks from where a Burlington kid might wear it.

gifts.name: Homeport
gifts.item: Third-floor science toys, games, or plush
gifts.why: The third floor of this four-story Church Street store is dedicated to toys, a detail regulars know and out-of-towners usually miss.

gifts.name: Vermont Lake Monsters
gifts.why: Wooden-bat collegiate baseball at Centennial Field, close enough to walk to from most of the Old North End, running from late May through early August.

categories.title: Under $25 and stocking stuffers
categories.intro: Small local things that still feel chosen on purpose.

gifts.name: Thirty-odd
gifts.why: Made by whichever of the shop's roughly 30 resident artists has stock that week — no two visits turn up the same sticker set.

gifts.name: Lake Champlain Chocolates
gifts.why: One bar, no box required — the almond, hazelnut, or fruit-and-nut varieties all ring in under the price of a movie ticket.

gifts.name: Pure Pop Records
gifts.why: Burlington's one remaining independent record shop stocks new and used vinyl side by side, so a $10 stocking stuffer and a $30 reissue sit in the same bin.

gifts.name: Darn Tough Vermont
gifts.where: Outdoor Gear Exchange, Skirack, or Common Deer
gifts.why: Every pair carries the company's unconditional lifetime guarantee — wear a hole in them and Northfield replaces them, no receipt required.

gifts.name: Skida
gifts.why: Thin enough for a spring run around the waterfront but still carries Skida's original Burlington-sewn construction.

gifts.name: Thirty-odd
gifts.why: Lets the recipient pick their own maker from the roughly 30 artists who stock the shop, rather than guessing at someone else's taste.

categories.title: The splurge
categories.intro: Burlington-made pieces and memberships with some staying power.

gifts.name: Queen City Footwear, via Common Deer
gifts.item: Queen City Dry Goods Carry All Bag in navy
gifts.why: Started as a made-to-measure shoe shop on Church Street in the late 1990s and grew into a full leather-goods line still cut and sewn in Burlington — the company rebranded in 2023, but the bags are the same shop's work.

gifts.name: AO Glass
gifts.item: Custom hand-blown glass piece or private studio session
gifts.why: Founded in 2007 by Rich Arentzen and Tove Ohlander, AO Glass launched an Immersive Glass Experience in 2026 where a recipient can watch — or make — a piece at 2,100°F in the working studio.

gifts.name: Conant Metal & Light
gifts.item: Custom or restored decorative lighting fixture
gifts.why: Steve Conant's team has built and restored metal lighting in Burlington for 40 years, tracing back to Conant Custom Brass before the 2008 relaunch — a fixture from here outlasts the house it hangs in.

gifts.name: Common Deer
gifts.why: A full Vermont-branded sweater, not a T-shirt souvenir — the kind of thing someone wears on purpose after they've lived here a few winters.

gifts.name: Shelburne Museum
gifts.why: A family membership covers the full 45-acre campus of historic buildings, folk art, and the steamboat Ticonderoga for a full year, plus reciprocal access to over 1,100 other museums nationwide.

categories.title: For the outdoorsy one
categories.intro: Gear, upkeep, and Vermont-made layers for getting outside.

gifts.name: Outdoor Gear Exchange
gifts.item: Gift card for new or used gear
gifts.why: OGE sells new gear on the main floor and consignment gear in the basement, so a gift card here can go toward a $600 kayak or a $40 pair of crampons depending on what the recipient actually needs.

gifts.name: Skida
gifts.where: Online, Skirack, or Outdoor Gear Exchange
gifts.why: Skida built its name in Nordic ski circles before expanding into everyday headwear, and the fleece-lined construction is still cut and sewn in the company's Burlington workshop.

gifts.name: Darn Tough Vermont
gifts.item: Hiking or ski sock, one pair
gifts.why: The lifetime guarantee matters more here than anywhere else on this list — someone who hikes or skis hard enough to wear through wool socks is exactly who that policy was written for.

gifts.name: Skirack
gifts.item: Bike tune-up gift certificate or apparel
gifts.why: Skirack has fitted ski boots and tuned bikes on Main Street since 1969, and a service certificate is a useful gift for someone who already owns the gear and just needs it maintained.

gifts.name: Turtle Fur
gifts.why: Family-owned since 1982 and still headquartered in Vermont, this is the original slouchy fleece hat the brand built its name on before it started making anything else.

categories.title: Made in the Old North End and South End studios
categories.intro: Work from local studios, galleries, and shops run by makers.

gifts.name: Thirty-odd
gifts.item: Handmade goods from about 30 resident makers
gifts.why: Founder Moe O'Hara, known for recycled and upcycled work, opened the shop inside the Soda Plant in 2018, and the makers who rent space here keep 100% of their own sales.

gifts.name: AO Glass
gifts.item: Hand-blown ornament, tumbler, or sun-catcher made on-site
gifts.why: The retail counter sits directly in front of the working furnace, so the piece someone buys was likely pulled from the fire earlier that same day.

gifts.name: Soapbox Arts
gifts.item: Contemporary art or a print by a featured artist
gifts.why: The gallery rotates shows of local and nationally recognized emerging artists inside a converted bottling plant, alongside Brio Coffeeworks and Thirty-odd in the same complex.

gifts.name: Frog Hollow Craft Gallery
gifts.item: Pottery, woodwork, textiles, or jewelry by Vermont artisans
gifts.why: Frog Hollow has represented more than 100 Vermont artisans out of this Church Street space since 1971, and still runs a monthly Craft Night open to the public.

gifts.name: Golden Hour Gift Co
gifts.item: Jewelry, clothing, or home goods from indie designers
gifts.why: The shop rebranded from Birdfolk Collective to Golden Hour Gift Co but kept its Winooski storefront and its focus on small, independent makers rather than wholesale brands.

categories.title: Experiences and gift cards
categories.intro: Tickets, memberships, and reasons to make a day of it.

gifts.name: The Flynn
gifts.item: Box office gift certificate or show tickets
gifts.why: The 1930s Art Deco theater on Main Street runs the region's main touring calendar of concerts, comedy, dance, and Broadway shows — check the calendar or call the box office for current gift certificate options.

gifts.name: ECHO, Leahy Center for Lake Champlain
gifts.why: A standing invitation to the lake-science exhibits and touch tanks on the Burlington waterfront, good for repeat rainy-day visits rather than a single afternoon.

gifts.name: Shelburne Museum
gifts.why: The museum runs a gift-membership discount promotion most Novembers, but the standard family rate already covers unlimited entry to the 45-acre grounds and its collection of relocated historic buildings.

gifts.name: Vermont Lake Monsters
gifts.price: $27+ for single games; call for mini-plans
gifts.why: The 2026 season runs from the May 30 home opener through an August 8 finale, and mini-plans are handled directly through the team's office rather than the online box office.

gifts.name: Generator
gifts.why: An 11,380-square-foot shop with a woodshop, metal shop, jewelry studio, and laser cutters under one roof — a useful gift for someone who keeps saying they want to learn to weld.

gifts.name: AO Glass
gifts.why: The studio's 2026 Immersive Glass Experience lets a visitor stamp a sun-catcher in 2,100°F molten glass themselves rather than just watching from behind the rope.
